title: Pentacalia tarapotensis
taxonomy: Pentacalia tarapotensis is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is a member of the genus Pentacalia, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The family Asteraceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, comprising over 23,000 species. Pentacalia tarapotensis is further classified as a shrub or subshrub, with its specific classification reflecting its unique characteristics and growth habits. The precise classification of Pentacalia tarapotensis may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic research.

The health benefits of Pentacalia tarapotensis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever, colds, and skin conditions.
